issues.title: display_name ?? email fallback repeated 5+ times in studio/seller-admins presentation layer
issues.description: The pattern `profile.display_name ?? profile.email` (and variants) is used 5+ times across three presentation components in apps/studio/src/features/seller-admins/presentation/components/. The logic represents a single business rule: 'resolve a user's display name, falling back to email'. It is not extracted to a shared utility within this feature (e.g., getDisplayName). The payments app already solved this correctly with `fetchUserDisplayNames`.
issues.rule: DRY - Rule of Three: same business rule used 7 times across 3 files in the same feature
issues.fix: Extract a utility function `getDisplayName(profile: { display_name: string | null; email: string }): string` to `apps/studio/src/features/seller-admins/domain/` or a shared application utility, then replace all inline usages.

issues.title: store/useSellerProfiles inlines DB query logic that could be extracted to an infrastructure utility
issues.description: apps/store/src/features/cart/application/hooks/useSellerProfiles.ts directly embeds the Supabase user_profiles query and display-name fallback logic (`display_name ?? email.split('@')[0]`) inside the hook function body. The payments app correctly separates this into `fetchUserDisplayNames` in the infrastructure layer. Within the store app, this is the only place the pattern is used — so it does not violate the Rule of Three by itself. However, the hook violates Clean Architecture by performing infrastructure (Supabase query) directly in the application layer hook instead of delegating to a query function.
issues.rule: DRY + Architecture: infrastructure concern (DB query) embedded in application layer hook; compare to payments app which correctly extracts to shared/infrastructure/fetchUserDisplayNames.ts
issues.fix: Extract the Supabase query to apps/store/src/features/cart/infrastructure/cartQueries.ts (or similar), and call it from the hook. This mirrors how payments handles the same concern.

patterns.description: payments app correctly extracts display name resolution to apps/payments/src/shared/infrastructure/fetchUserDisplayNames.ts and reuses it across checkoutQueries.ts and orderQueries.ts — good example of DRY at the infrastructure layer.

SELLER_PROFILES_QUERY_KEY = 'seller-profiles' appears in both store and payments apps with the same string value. This is acceptable coincidental duplication across separate app runtimes — each app owns its own query keys independently.
TEMPLATES_QUERY_KEY = 'product-templates' appears in both admin and studio apps. Same rationale — separate runtimes, coincidental same value.
